Off Calendar Feb-03-2022 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-01-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2021 Continued To Jun-13-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ian Eliasoph, Esq. (415.554.3864; ian.eliasoph@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Defendants.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ROSS MIRKARIMI and VICKI HENNESSEY'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendants' (City and County of San Francisco, Ross Mirkarimi, and Vicki Hennessey) demurrer to Plaintiff Douglas S. Jones Jr.'s third amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred by the litigation privilege. (Cal. Civ. Code sec. 47.)

"The usual formulation is that the privilege applies to any communication (1) made in judicial or quasi-judicial proceedings; (2) by litigants or other participants authorized by law; (3) to achieve the objects of the litigation; and (4) that have some connection or logical relation to the action." (Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal.3d 205, 212.) "Any doubt about whether the privilege applies is resolved in favor of applying it." (Kashian v. Harriman (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 892, 913.) The privilege also "extends to noncommunicative acts that are necessarily related to the communicative conduct unless it is demonstrated that an independent, noncommunicative, wrongful act was the gravamen of the action, the litigation privilege applies." (Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1065.)

The gravamen of Plaintiff's claims is that a letter Defendants sent to Plaintiff stating that they would "not accept the arbitrator's recommendation for reinstatement, back pay, or the reduction of discipline to a written reprimand" (Pl.'s RJN, Ex. 3, pp. 3-4) constitutes retaliation and discrimination. As such, "the injury allegedly resulted from an act that was communicative in its essential nature." (Rusheen, 37 Cal.4th at 1058.) Defendants made this communication following quasi-judicial arbitration proceedings to achieve their objective in those proceedings-upholding Plaintiff's termination. Accordingly, Plaintiff's claims fall squarely within the litigation privilege. (See Optional Capital, Inc. v. Akin Gump Struss, Hauer & Feld LLP (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 95, 118 [defendants met their burden of showing that litigation privilege applied because the communicative conduct at issue was made in judicial or quasi-judicial proceedings by attorneys to achieve the object of the proceedings and had some connection or logical relation to the action]; see also Rusheen, 37 Cal.4th at 1057 ["communications with some relation to judicial proceedings are absolutely immune from tort liability by the litigation privilege. It is not limited to statements made during a trial or other proceedings, but may extend to steps taken prior thereto, or afterwards." (internal quotations and citations omitted)].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DUSHAWN JOHNSON, Esq. of Law Office of Dushawn Johnson (Tel: 559-643-4262) for plaintiff;
Atty. IAN H. ELIASOPH, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3864) for defendants City and County of San Francisco, Ross Mirkarimi, Vicki Hennessey.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling but will remove mention of the demurrer being unopposed, as follows:

The City and County of San Francisco's demurrers are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition was untimely. (CCP 1005.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF") motion for judgment on the pleadings on plaintiff's First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 1 and 7, without leave to amend as to causes of action 2-5 as conceded, and with 15 days leave to amend as to cause of action 6.

(see word mintues for complete details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek , Not Reported (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Continued To Sep-13-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for judgment on the pleadings is continued to September 13, 2021. Defendant shall re-serve on plaintiff by electronic mail exhibits A-G to its request for judicial notice, which were previously served on Plaintiff in connection with its demurrer. Plaintiff's opposition is now due on August 30, 2021.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
